Students listened, memorized, and repeated them repeatedly.
This oral tradition kept the knowledge alive for centuries.
examples.illustration: Guru speaking -> Shishya listening and repeating

examples.question: What can we learn from a Megalith burial site?
steps: Items found with the body tell us about the person's occupation or wealth.
Circle of stones on top indicates it was a burial marker.

ss_his_5.what: Rise of Mahajanapadas (large kingdoms) like Magadha and republics like Vajji.
ss_his_5.example: Magadha became powerful because it was surrounded by rivers and iron ore mines.
points: Rajas realized the importance of taxes to maintain armies
Fortifications (walls) were built around major cities
Magadha had two very powerful rulers: Bimbisara and Ajatashatru
ss_his_5.tip: Vajji was a Gana or Sangha, which means it was ruled by a group of people!
ss_his_5.question: What was the capital of Magadha?

examples.question: Why was Magadha more successful than other Mahajanapadas?
steps: It was surrounded by the Ganga and Son rivers (transport and water).
It had iron ore mines to make strong weapons and tools.
It had thick forests nearby providing timber and elephants for the army.

examples.question: What was the role of Rajagriha in Magadha's history?
steps: It was the first capital of Magadha.
It was surrounded by five hills making it very safe from enemies.

ss_his_6.what: Rise of new religious ideas by Siddhartha (Buddha) and Mahavira, and the philosophy of Upanishads.
ss_his_6.example: The story of Kisagotami teaches us about the inevitability of death.
points: Buddhism teaches the Eightfold Path to end suffering
Jainism emphasizes Ahimsa (non-violence) for all living beings
The Upanishads discuss the nature of the soul (Atman) and Brahman
ss_his_6.tip: Buddhacharita is a famous biography of the Buddha!
ss_his_6.question: What was the original name of the Buddha?

examples.question: Why did Buddhism become popular among common people?
steps: Buddha taught in Prakrit, the language of the common people.
He preached equality and rejected the strict caste system.
He focused on kindness (Ahimsa) and simple living.
examples.illustration: Buddha teaching thousands of people under a tree

examples.question: What is the meaning of "Upanishad"?
steps: Actually means "sitting near" a teacher to learn.
It recording discussions between teachers and students about the soul.

ss_his_7.what: Ashoka was a unique Mauryan ruler who gave up war after the Kalinga battle and spread Dhamma.
ss_his_7.example: Ashoka's messages are still found on pillars across India.
points: Ashoka used Prakrit language so common people could understand
He appointed Dhamma Mahamattas to teach people ethical living
The lion capital on our currency is from Ashoka's pillar at Sarnath
ss_his_7.tip: Ashoka was the first king in world history to give up conquest after winning a war.
ss_his_7.question: Which battle changed Ashoka's life?

examples.question: Why did Ashoka stop fighting after the Kalinga war?
steps: He saw thousands of deaths and suffering on both sides.
He felt deep sorrow and realized that victory through force is not true victory.
He decided to conquer people's hearts through "Dhamma".
examples.illustration: Ashoka looking at a battlefield with sadness

examples.question: Give an example of Ashoka's Dhamma message.
steps: "Respect your parents and elders."
"Be kind to your servants and animals."
"Do not criticize other people's religions."

ss_his_8.what: Growth of towns and trade led to prosperous villages and the rise of the Silk Route.
ss_his_8.example: Ancient Indian spices and silk were very famous in the Roman Empire.
points: Guilds (shrenis) were groups of artisans and merchants
Iron tools like the ploughshare increased agricultural yield
South India was famous for gold, spices, and precious stones
ss_his_8.tip: The Silk Route was a network of trade routes connecting China to the West!
ss_his_8.question: What was a "Shreni" in ancient India?

examples.question: How did the use of iron tools change farming?
steps: Iron ploughshares could dig deeper than wooden ones.
Farmers could prepare tough soil for better crops.
It led to more food production and the growth of towns.
examples.illustration: Iron plough = More food = Bigger towns

examples.question: What would a traveling merchant carry along the Silk Route?
steps: Silk from China, cotton from India, spices and precious stones.
They also carried new ideas and religions back and forth.

ss_his_9.what: After the Mauryas, new dynasties like the Guptas and Pallavas established powerful kingdoms.
ss_his_9.example: The Prayag Prashasti tells us about the great conquests of Samudragupta.
points: Harshavardhana was a famous king of Thanesar
The Guptas were known for great developments in science and art
The Pallavas and Chalukyas were major powers in South India
ss_his_9.tip: Prashasti means "in praise of" in Sanskrit!
ss_his_9.question: Who was the court poet of Samudragupta?

examples.question: What is a Prashasti? Give an example.
steps: It means "In Praise Of".
Samudragupta's prashasti on the Allahabad Pillar is a famous example.
It lists his bravery and how he defeated many kings.
examples.illustration: Tall pillar with carved Sanskrit poems

examples.question: How did Samudragupta treat kings of South India?
steps: He defeated them in war but allowed them to rule again.
They had to pay him tribute and accept his authority.

ss_his_10.what: Ancient India was a hub of great architecture (temples, stupas) and literature.
ss_his_10.example: The Sanchi Stupa is a world-famous Buddhist monument.
points: Kalidasa wrote the famous play Abhijnanashakuntalam
Aryabhata was a great mathematician and astronomer
Puranas and Epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata were compiled
ss_his_10.tip: The Iron Pillar at Mehrauli has not rusted even after 1500 years!
ss_his_10.question: Who wrote the book "Aryabhatiyam"?

examples.question: Describe a Stupa.
steps: It is a mound-like structure (Anda) built over sacred relics.
It has a walkway (Pradakshina patha) for people to go around it.
The Sanchi Stupa is one of the most famous examples.

examples.question: Why is the Mehrauli Iron Pillar a wonder of science?
steps: It is over 1500 years old but has not rusted at all.
It shows that ancient Indians were masters of metallurgy (working with metal).

ss_geo_1.what: The Earth is one of the eight planets in the solar system, orbiting the Sun.
ss_geo_1.example: Looking at the North Star (Pole Star) helps travelers find direction at night.
points: The Sun is at the center of the solar system
The Milky Way is the galaxy which includes our solar system
Earth is the only planet known to support life
ss_geo_1.tip: Mercury is the closest planet to the sun, but Venus is the hottest!
ss_geo_1.question: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?

examples.question: Why is life possible only on Earth in our solar system?
steps: Earth has the right distance from the sun (not too hot or cold).
It has air with oxygen to breathe.
It has precious water in liquid form.
examples.illustration: Earth = Blue Marble with clouds and oceans

examples.question: Identify the inner and outer planets.
steps: Inner (rocky): Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars.
Outer (gaseous/big): Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune.

ss_geo_1.textbookContent: Chapter 1 in Geography covers "The Earth in the Solar System". It explains celestial bodies like the sun, moon, and stars. It distinguishes between Planets (no heat/light of their own) and Stars (Luminous). It also defines the "Full Moon" (Purnima) and "New Moon" (Amavasya).

qa.q: Why is Venus called Earth's twin?
qa.a: Because its size and shape are very similar to those of the Earth.
An orbit is the fixed, elliptical path along which planets revolve around the sun.

ss_geo_2.what: Latitudes and longitudes are imaginary lines on the globe used to locate any place.
ss_geo_2.example: The Equator is the 0° latitude that divides Earth into two halves.
points: The Prime Meridian (0° longitude) passes through Greenwich
The area between the Tropics is the hottest zone (Torrid Zone)
Standard Time in India is based on the 82°30' E longitude
ss_geo_2.tip: Latitudes are like rungs on a ladder; Longitudes go from pole to pole!
ss_geo_2.question: What is the latitude of the North Pole?

examples.question: Find the time difference if you move 15 degrees east of Greenwich.
steps: Earth rotates 1 degree in 4 minutes.
15 degrees x 4 = 60 minutes.
So, the time would be 1 hour ahead of Greenwich.

examples.question: Where is the Torrid Zone located?
steps: It is the area between the Tropic of Cancer (23.5° N) and the Tropic of Capricorn (23.5° S).
It receives direct sunlight and is the hottest zone.

ss_geo_3.what: The Earth has two motions: Rotation (spinning on axis) and Revolution (moving around the sun).
ss_geo_3.example: Rotation causes day and night; Revolution causes seasons.
points: Earth takes 24 hours for one rotation
It takes 365.25 days for one revolution (leap year every 4 years)
The tilting of the Earth's axis is why we have different seasons
ss_geo_3.tip: A leap year has 366 days, with an extra day in February!
ss_geo_3.question: What causes day and night on Earth?

examples.question: Why do we have different seasons?
steps: Earth revolves around the sun on a tilted axis.
When the North Pole tilts toward the sun, it is summer there.
When it tilts away, it is winter.

examples.question: What is an Equinox?
steps: Happens on March 21 and Sep 23.
The whole world has equal length of day and night because sunlight falls directly on the equator.

ss_geo_4.what: Maps are flat representations of the Earth or parts of it, including physical, political, and thematic types.
ss_geo_4.example: A road map of your city is a thematic map.
points: Physical maps show natural features like mountains and rivers
Political maps show boundaries of countries and states
Symbols and colors represent different things on a map
ss_geo_4.tip: The top of a map is almost always North!
ss_geo_4.question: What type of map shows rainfall or industries?

examples.question: How to calculate distance on a map using a scale?
steps: Find the scale (e.g., 1 cm = 100 km).
Measure distance between two cities on map (e.g., 5 cm).
Multiply: 5 x 100 = 500 km.
examples.illustration: Ruler measure x Scale = Real distance

examples.question: What do the colors Blue, Brown, and Green represent on a map?
steps: Blue: Water bodies.
Brown: Mountains/Highlands.
Green: Plains/Vegetation.

ss_geo_5.what: The four major spheres of Earth: Lithosphere (land), Hydrosphere (water), Atmosphere (air), and Biosphere (life).
ss_geo_5.example: The Biosphere is the narrow zone where land, water, and air interact to support life.
points: Asia is the largest continent on Earth
The Pacific is the largest and deepest ocean
Nitrogen and Oxygen are the main gases in the atmosphere
ss_geo_5.tip: Mount Everest is the highest point on the Lithosphere!
ss_geo_5.question: Which is the largest continent on Earth?

examples.question: Identify the highest peak and the deepest ocean point.
steps: Highest: Mt. Everest (Lithosphere) - 8848m.
Deepest: Mariana Trench (Hydrosphere) - over 11,000m.

examples.question: Name the gas that helps traps heat and keeps Earth warm.
steps: Carbon Dioxide (CO2).
In small amounts, it is essential but too much causes Global Warming.

ss_geo_6.what: Major landforms include mountains, plateaus, and plains formed by internal and external processes.
ss_geo_6.example: The Himalayas are young fold mountains.
points: Plateaus are flat-topped tablelands like the Deccan Plateau
Plains are fertile lowlands formed by river deposits
Block mountains form when large areas are broken and displaced
ss_geo_6.tip: Most of the world's population lives in plains because they are easy to farm!
ss_geo_6.question: Name a plateau in India.

examples.question: Difference between a Fold Mountain and a Block Mountain.
steps: Fold: Formed by wrinkling of Earth's surface (Himalayas).
Block: Formed when large areas break and lift up (Vindhyas).

examples.question: Why are plateaus called "storehouses of minerals"?
steps: The Deccan Plateau in India is rich in iron, coal, and manganese deposits.
The African plateau is famous for gold and diamonds.

ss_geo_7.what: India is a vast country with diverse physical features like the Himalayas, the Thar Desert, and the Coastal Plains.
ss_geo_7.example: India is surrounded by the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and the Indian Ocean.
points: The Tropic of Cancer passes almost through the middle of India
India has 28 states and 8 union territories
The Himalayas mean the "Abode of Snow"
ss_geo_7.tip: India is the 7th largest country in the world by area!
ss_geo_7.question: Which ocean is named after India?

examples.question: Identify India's neighbors to the North and South.
steps: North: China, Nepal, Bhutan.
South: Sri Lanka and Maldives.
examples.illustration: Map of India with surrounding countries

examples.question: What are the three main ranges of the Himalayas?
steps: Himadri (Great Himalayas) - highest.
Himachal (Middle Himalayas).
Shivalik (Outer Himalayas) - lowest.

ss_geo_8.what: Exploring India's tropical monsoon climate, diverse forests, and rich wildlife.
ss_geo_8.example: The Gir forest in Gujarat is the only home of the Asiatic lion.
points: Monsoon rains are vital for Indian agriculture
Evergreen forests stay green all year round
Wildlife sanctuaries protect endangered animals
ss_geo_8.tip: Trees like Neem and Tulsi have great medicinal value!
ss_geo_8.question: What type of climate does India have?

examples.question: Contrast Evergreen and Deciduous forests.
steps: Evergreen: Trees shed leaves at different times, so never appear bare.
Deciduous: Trees shed leaves all at once in a specific dry season.

examples.question: Why is the lion "Project Tiger" important?
steps: It was launched to save the declining population of tigers in India.
It helps protect the whole forest ecosystem.

ss_civ_1.what: Diversity means variety — in language, religion, food, and culture. India is a land of great diversity.
ss_civ_1.example: In your class, friends might speak different languages at home but study together.
points: Unity in Diversity is a phrase coined by Jawaharlal Nehru
Historical and geographical factors influence diversity
Diversity enriches our lives by exposing us to new perspectives
ss_civ_1.tip: Despite our differences, we are all part of one big family — India!
ss_civ_1.question: Who coined the phrase "Unity in Diversity"?

examples.question: How does diversity help in a sports team?
steps: Players from different backgrounds bring different strengths and strategies.
It teaches teammates to respect and welcome new ideas.
examples.illustration: Team of diverse players working together

examples.question: How did historical factors lead to diversity in Ladakh?
steps: Being on a trade route, it welcomed people from Tibet and Central Asia.
It became a place where Buddhism and Islam coexist peacefully.

ss_civ_2.what: Prejudice and stereotypes lead to discrimination. Equality is a fundamental right in democracy.
ss_civ_2.example: Saying "boys don't cry" is a common gender stereotype.
points: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar fought against the caste system in India
The Constitution ensures equality for all citizens
Untouchability has been abolished by law
ss_civ_2.tip: Treating everyone with respect is the first step toward equality.
ss_civ_2.question: Who is known as the Father of the Indian Constitution?

examples.question: What is the main difference between a stereotype and a prejudice?
steps: Prejudice is a negative opinion about someone before knowing them.
Stereotype is fitting a whole group into one fixed image (e.g., "girls are weak").
examples.illustration: Prejudice = "I don't like them" ; Stereotype = "They are all like this"

examples.question: Mention a constitutional right that promotes equality.
steps: Untouchability is an offense and has been abolished by law.
Every citizen has equal opportunity for jobs and education.

ss_civ_3.what: Government is a group of people that governs a country or state, making rules and decisions.
ss_civ_3.example: The government builds roads and ensures electricity for everyone.
points: Democracy is rule by the people through representatives
Monarchy is rule by a king or queen
Government works at three levels: Local, State, and National
ss_civ_3.tip: Universal Adult Franchise means all adults can vote, regardless of their background.
ss_civ_3.question: What is the minimum age to vote in India?

examples.question: Why is the voting age 18 in India?
steps: By 18, citizens are considered mature enough to understand their choices.
It ensures that the government is chosen by adults who are responsible for their actions.

examples.question: Identify two main types of government.
steps: Democracy: People choose their leaders.
Monarchy: One family rules through generations.

ss_civ_4.what: Democracy works through public participation, resolution of conflict, and ensuring equality and justice.
ss_civ_4.example: People protest or sign petitions if they don't like a government decision.
points: Regular elections allow people to change the government
Conflicts over river water or religion are resolved by law
Equality and justice are the pillars of democratic government
ss_civ_4.tip: The police and courts play a huge role in resolving societal conflicts.
ss_civ_4.question: How often are general elections held in India?

examples.question: How do people participate in a democracy other than voting?
steps: By joining peaceful protests or rallies.
By writing letters to ministers or signing petitions.
By taking part in television debates and discussions.

examples.question: How are river water disputes solved?
steps: The Central government steps in to settle disputes between two states.
Courts might be involved to find a fair sharing system.

ss_civ_5.what: The local government in villages, consisting of the Gram Sabha and the Gram Panchayat.
ss_civ_5.example: A village Sarpanch leading a meeting to fix the village well.
points: Gram Sabha is a meeting of all adults in a village
Gram Panchayat is the executive body of the village
Panchayati Raj has three levels: Village, Block, and District
ss_civ_5.tip: Gram Sabha is the best example of direct democracy at the root level!
ss_civ_5.question: Who is the head of the Gram Panchayat?

examples.question: Who can be a member of the Gram Sabha?
steps: Any adult (18+) who has the right to vote.
They must live in the area covered by the Panchayat.
examples.illustration: Village meeting under a large tree

examples.question: How does the Gram Panchayat get its money?
steps: Taxes on houses, marketplaces, and fairs.
Government grants from the district and state.

ss_civ_6.what: Key officials like the Patwari and the SHO ensure peace and record-keeping in rural areas.
ss_civ_6.example: The Patwari measuring land to resolve a boundary dispute between farmers.
points: Patwari maintains land records and collects revenue
SHO (Station House Officer) is the head of the local police station
District Collector is the head of the entire district administration
ss_civ_6.tip: The Hindu Succession Amendment Act (2005) gives equal property rights to daughters.
ss_civ_6.question: What is the main job of a Patwari?

examples.question: If a farmer wants to see his land map, whom should he go to?
steps: He should visit the local Patwari.
The Patwari maintains records of all land holdings in the area.

examples.question: What can you do if there is a theft in your house in a village?
steps: Go to the nearest police station.
Ask the SHO to register an FIR (First Information Report).

ss_civ_7.what: Urban areas are managed by Municipal Corporations (big cities) or Municipal Councils (smaller towns).
ss_civ_7.example: Garbage collection trucks and street lights are managed by the municipality.
points: Ward Councillors are elected representatives for small city wards
The Commissioner and administrative staff implement decisions
Protests like "The Surat cleaning drive" show community power
ss_civ_7.tip: Elected councillors make the policies; appointed officials carry them out.
ss_civ_7.question: What is the head of a Municipal Corporation called?

examples.question: Why are cities divided into wards?
steps: Cities are too big for one person to manage.
Division into wards makes it easy to attend to local problems like street lights or drainage.
examples.illustration: City map divided into color-coded Wards

examples.question: Where does the Municipal Corporation get money from?
steps: Property tax, water tax, and fees for building permissions.
State government fund support.

ss_civ_8.what: Farmers, laborers, and artisans earn their living in villages through agriculture and related work.
ss_civ_8.example: Thulasi is a landless laborer who works on other people's farms for daily wages.
points: Small farmers often fall into debt to buy seeds and fertilizers
Terrace farming in hilly areas like Nagaland saves water
Fishing is a major livelihood in coastal villages
ss_civ_8.tip: Most rural families depend on agriculture, but many also do pottery or weaving.
ss_civ_8.question: What is the main problem faced by small farmers?

examples.question: What are the main causes of debt for small farmers?
steps: High cost of seeds and fertilizers.
Failure of crops due to lack of rain or pests.
Taking loans at very high interest rates from moneylenders.
examples.illustration: Loan cycle = Seeds -> No Rain -> Debt

examples.question: Identify a non-farming occupation in rural areas.
steps: Pottery, basket weaving, or working in a local sugar mill.

ss_civ_9.what: In cities, people work in factories, offices, and as street vendors or shopkeepers.
ss_civ_9.example: Bachchu Manjhi is a cycle-rickshaw puller who moved from a village to the city for work.
points: Self-employed people like vendors have no job security
Permanent employees get benefits like medical leave and pensions
Casual workers are hired only when there is more work
ss_civ_9.tip: Service sector (IT, banking) is growing fast in Indian cities!
ss_civ_9.question: Name one benefit of a permanent job compared to a casual job.

examples.question: Difference between a self-employed person and a salaried person.
steps: Self-employed: Owns the business, no fixed income (e.g., tailor).
Salaried: Works for a company, gets fixed monthly pay and benefits.

examples.question: Mention a challenge faced by street vendors.
steps: They have no permanent space to work.
Police might ask them to move their stalls at any time.

hin_vas_1.what: यह कविता एक नन्हीं नीले पंखों वाली चिड़िया के बारे में है जो संतोषी है और प्रकृति से प्यार करती है।
hin_vas_1.example: जैसे चिड़िया को नदी और अन्न से प्यार है, क्या आपको भी प्रकृति पसंद है?
points: चिड़िया को एकांत में गाना बहुत पसंद है
वह संकट के समय भी हिम्मत नहीं हारती
यह कविता हमें संतोषी बनने की सीख देती है
hin_vas_1.tip: अपनी छोटी-छोटी खुशियों में आनंद ढूंढना सीखें!
hin_vas_1.question: चिड़िया को किन-किन चीज़ों से प्यार है?

examples.question: चिड़िया को "संतोषी" क्यों कहा गया है?
steps: वह थोड़े से दाने खाकर भी ख़ुश रहती है।
वह नदी के बीच से मोती रूपी जल लाती है जिससे उसका मान बढ़ता है।
examples.illustration: नीले पंखों वाली नन्हीं चिड़िया का चित्र

examples.question: चिड़िया किन चीज़ों से बहुत प्यार करती है?
steps: उसे अन्न (अनाज के दाने) से बहुत प्यार है।
उसे विजन (एकांत) और नदी से बहुत गहरा लगाव है।

hin_vas_2.what: लेखिका कृष्णा सोबती अपने बचपन की यादें साझा करती हैं और बताती हैं कि तब और अब के समय में कितना बदलाव आया है।
hin_vas_2.example: अपने दादा-दादी से पूछें कि उनके बचपन में उनके पसंदीदा खिलौने क्या थे।
points: पुराने समय में ग्रामोफोन होते थे, रेडियो और टेलीविज़न नहीं
लेखिका को इतवार की सुबह अपने मोज़े खुद धोने पड़ते थे
समय के साथ खाने-पीने और पहनावे में भी बहुत बदलाव आया है
hin_vas_2.tip: बड़ों के अनुभवों से सीखना हमेशा दिलचस्प होता है!
hin_vas_2.question: लेखिका बचपन में इतवार की सुबह क्या-क्या काम करती थी?

examples.question: लेखिका के समय की कुल्फी अब किस चीज़ में बदल गई है?
steps: लेखिका जब बच्ची थीं तो कुल्फी होती थी।
आजकल वह कुल्फी आइसक्रीम (Ice-cream) में बदल गई है।
examples.illustration: कुल्फी का चित्र बनाम आधुनिक आइसक्रीम

examples.question: लेखिका को शनिवार को क्या कड़वी चीज़ पीनी पड़ती थी?
steps: लेखिका को तंदुरुस्ती के लिए शनिवार को "कैस्टर ऑयल" या "ओलिव ऑयल" पीना पड़ता था।

hin_vas_3.what: केशव और श्यामा दो भाई-बहन हैं जो चिड़िया के अंडों की हिफाजत करना चाहते हैं, लेकिन अपनी नादानी में उन्हें खराब कर देते हैं।
hin_vas_3.example: कभी-कभी हम किसी की मदद करना चाहते हैं पर जानकारी के अभाव में नुकसान कर बैठते हैं।
points: बच्चों को लगा कि अंडों को धूप और भूख से बचाना चाहिए
उन्हें यह नहीं पता था कि छूने से चिड़िया के अंडे अपवित्र हो जाते हैं
यह कहानी बाल सुलभ जिज्ञासा और नादानी को दर्शाती है
hin_vas_3.tip: जीव-जंतुओं की मदद करने से पहले उनके बारे में सही जानकारी ले लेनी चाहिए।
hin_vas_3.question: केशव ने अंडों के लिए कार्निस पर क्या-क्या चीज़ें रखी थीं?

examples.question: अंडों को छूने के बाद चिड़िया ने क्या किया और क्यों?
steps: चिड़िया ने अंडों को घोंसले से नीचे गिरा दिया।
चिड़िया के अनुसार इंसान के छूने से अंडे गंदे हो जाते हैं और वह फिर उन्हें नहीं सेती।
examples.illustration: केशव का उदास चेहरा और टूटे हुए अंडे

examples.question: श्यामा ने केशव की क्या मदद की थी?
steps: श्यामा ने स्टूल के नीचे पुरानी धोती की गद्दी बनाकर दी थी।
वह दरवाज़े पर नज़र रख रही थी कि कहीं अम्मा न आ जाएँ।

hin_vas_4.what: एक १०-११ साल की बच्ची चाँद को हाथ लगाकर उससे बातें कर रही है और उसकी घटती-बढ़ती आकृति पर अपनी कल्पना व्यक्त कर रही है।
hin_vas_4.example: पूर्णिमा के चाँद को देखकर आपको क्या कल्पना आती है?
points: बच्ची को लगता है कि चाँद ने तारों जड़ा आकाश रूपी वस्त्र पहना है
चांद को कोई बीमारी है जिससे वह घटता और बढ़ता रहता है
यह कविता बच्चों की मासूम सोच को दर्शाती है
hin_vas_4.tip: कल्पना की कोई सीमा नहीं होती, चाँद को देखकर अपनी कहानी बनाएं!
hin_vas_4.question: बच्ची के अनुसार चाँद को क्या बीमारी है?

hin_vas_5.what: अक्षरों की खोज मानव सभ्यता की सबसे बड़ी खोज है। इसके बिना हम अपना इतिहास नहीं जान पाते।
hin_vas_5.example: सोचिए अगर अक्षर न होते तो हम एक-दूसरे को अपनी बातें लिखकर कैसे बताते?
points: दुनिया की सभी पुस्तकें अक्षरों से बनी हैं
अक्षरों की खोज से पहले मनुष्य चित्रों के माध्यम से अपनी बात कहता था
अक्षरों की खोज लगभग ६००० साल पहले हुई थी
hin_vas_5.tip: लिखना और पढ़ना हमारे ज्ञान के द्वार खोलता है!
hin_vas_5.question: अक्षरों की खोज से मनुष्य को क्या फायदा हुआ?

hin_vas_6.what: यह एक काल्पनिक कहानी है जो मंगल ग्रह के निवासियों के बारे में है जो ज़मीन के नीचे रहते हैं।
hin_vas_6.example: क्या आपने कभी सोचा है कि दूसरे ग्रहों पर जीवन कैसा होगा?
points: छोटू अपने पापा का सुरक्षा पास लेकर सुरंग में चला जाता है
मंगल ग्रह पर पहले लोग ऊपर रहते थे, पर सौर मंडल में बदलाव के कारण नीचे चले गए
वैज्ञानिक यंत्रों की मदद से ग्रह की स्थिति पर नज़र रखते हैं
hin_vas_6.tip: विज्ञान और तकनीक हमें वहां तक ले जा सकती है जहाँ हम सोच भी नहीं सकते।
hin_vas_6.question: छोटू के पापा को सुरंग में जाने की अनुमति क्यों थी?

hin_vas_7.what: यह गीत मिलकर काम करने और एकता की शक्ति का संदेश देता है।
hin_vas_7.example: यदि हम सब मिलकर एक भारी पत्थर को उठाएँ, तो काम आसान हो जाएगा।
points: एक अकेला व्यक्ति थक सकता है, इसलिए साथ मिलकर काम करना चाहिए
मेहनत करने वालों के आगे पर्वत भी शीश झुकाते हैं
हमारी मंज़िल सच की मंज़िल है और रास्ता नेक है
hin_vas_7.tip: टीम वर्क (Team Work) से बड़े से बड़ा लक्ष्य प्राप्त किया जा सकता है!
hin_vas_7.question: इस गीत में "फौलादी सीने और बाहें" किसके लिए कहा गया है?

hin_vas_8.what: यह एक मज़ेदार नाटक है जिसमें एक बच्चा स्कूल जाने से बचने के लिए "ऐसे-ऐसे" बीमारी का बहाना बनाता है।
hin_vas_8.example: क्या आपने कभी होमवर्क न करने पर पेट दर्द का बहाना बनाया है?
points: मोहन के पेट में अजीब सा दर्द हो रहा था जिसे वह "ऐसे-ऐसे" कह रहा था
वैद्य जी और डॉक्टर भी उसकी बीमारी का कारण नहीं समझ पा रहे थे
अंत में मास्टर जी ने राज खोला कि यह केवल होमवर्क न करने का डर है
hin_vas_8.tip: ईमानदारी और समय पर काम करना ही सर्वश्रेष्ठ है!
hin_vas_8.question: मोहन की बीमारी का असली कारण क्या था?

examples.question: मास्टर जी ने मोहन की बीमारी का क्या इलाज बताया?
steps: उन्होंने कहा कि मोहन को "ऐसे-ऐसे" की दवा नहीं, बल्कि छुट्टी के दो दिन चाहिए।
उन्होंने मोहन को स्कूल का अधूरा काम पूरा करने का आदेश दिया।
examples.illustration: मास्टर जी मुस्कुराते हुए और मोहन का डर

examples.question: मोहन के माता-पिता क्यों घबरा गए थे?
steps: उन्हें लगा कि मोहन को सच में कोई गंभीर पेट दर्द है जिसे डॉक्टर भी नहीं समझ पा रहे।

hin_vas_9.what: राजप्पा और नागराजन की टिकट एलबम को लेकर प्रतिस्पर्धा और ईर्ष्या की कहानी।
hin_vas_9.example: प्रतियोगिता अच्छी है, पर ईर्ष्या हमें गलत रास्ता दिखा सकती है।
points: नागराजन को उसके मामा ने सिंगापुर से एक सुंदर एलबम भेजा था
राजप्पा को अपनी मेहनत से बनाई एलबम की उपेक्षा अखर रही थी
अंत में राजप्पा को अपनी गलती (चोरी) का बहुत पछतावा होता है
hin_vas_9.tip: दूसरों की सफलता पर खुश होना सीखें, इससे मन को शांति मिलती है।
hin_vas_9.question: राजप्पा ने नागराजन की एलबम का क्या किया?

hin_vas_10.what: यह कविता १८५७ के स्वतंत्रता संग्राम की वीरांगना रानी लक्ष्मीबाई के बलिदान और वीरता की गाथा है।
hin_vas_10.example: रानी लक्ष्मीबाई का नाम सुनते ही हमारे मन में साहस भर जाता है।
points: महारानी लक्ष्मीबाई ने बचपन में ही शस्त्र चलाना सीख लिया था
उन्होंने अंग्रेज़ों के दांत खट्टे कर दिए और वीरगति को प्राप्त हुईं
यह कविता देशभक्ति की अमर भावना का प्रतीक है
hin_vas_10.tip: वीरता केवल पुरुषों की विरासत नहीं है, नारी भी रणचंडी बन सकती है!
hin_vas_10.question: रानी लक्ष्मीबाई की बचपन की सहेलियाँ कौन थीं?

hin_ram_1.what: भगवान राम का अवधपुरी (अयोध्या) में जन्म और उनके बचपन की कथा।
hin_ram_1.example: अयोध्या सरयू नदी के किनारे स्थित एक बहुत सुंदर नगरी थी।
points: राजा दशरथ के चार पुत्र थे: राम, भरत, लक्ष्मण और शत्रुघ्न
ऋषि विश्वामित्र राम और लक्ष्मण को अपने साथ ले जाने के लिए आए
राम का स्वभाव अत्यंत शांत और आज्ञाकारी था
hin_ram_1.tip: मर्यादा पुरुषोत्तम राम के जीवन से हमें अनुशासन की सीख मिलती है।
hin_ram_1.question: ऋषि विश्वामित्र राम को अपने साथ क्यों ले जाना चाहते थे?

hin_ram_2.what: राम और लक्ष्मण की विश्वामित्र के साथ यात्रा, राक्षसों का वध और माता सीता से विवाह।
hin_ram_2.example: जनकपुर में राम ने शिव का धनुष तोड़कर सीता स्वयंवर जीता।
points: राम ने ताड़का और सुबाहु जैसे राक्षसों का अंत किया
राम को विश्वामित्र ने कई दिव्य अस्त्र और विद्याएँ दीं
राजा जनक की पुत्री सीता का विवाह राम से हुआ
hin_ram_2.tip: बुराई पर अच्छाई की जीत हमेशा निश्चित होती है।
hin_ram_2.question: शिव धनुष तोड़ने पर क्या हुआ?

hin_gr_1.what: संज्ञा किसी व्यक्ति, वस्तु या स्थान का नाम है। सर्वनाम वह शब्द है जो संज्ञा के स्थान पर प्रयुक्त होता है।
hin_gr_1.example: राम (संज्ञा) घर गया। वह (सर्वनाम) सो गया।
points: संज्ञा के भेद: व्यक्तिवाचक, जातिवाचक और भाववाचक
सर्वनाम भाषा को सरल और सुंदर बनाते हैं
जैसे: मैं, तुम, वह, हम, आप
hin_gr_1.tip: भाववाचक संज्ञा हमारी भावनाओं को व्यक्त करती है जैसे खुशी या क्रोध।
hin_gr_1.question: संज्ञा के किन्हीं तीन उदाहरणों के नाम बताएं।

examples.question: "हिमालय बहुत ऊँचा पर्वत है" - इस वाक्य में संज्ञा शब्द पहचानें।
steps: हिमालय (व्यक्तिवाचक संज्ञा) क्योंकि यह एक विशेष पर्वत का नाम है।
पर्वत (जातिवाचक संज्ञा) क्योंकि यह पूरी पर्वत श्रेणी को दर्शाता है।

examples.question: "वह जयपुर जा रहा है" - यहाँ सर्वनाम शब्द कौन सा है?
steps: "वह" शब्द सर्वनाम है क्योंकि यह किसी व्यक्ति के नाम (संज्ञा) की जगह आया है।

comp_1.what: A computer is a smart electronic machine that takes data, processes it, and gives useful output.
comp_1.example: Your smartphone is actually a very powerful small computer!
points: Computer follows Input -> Process -> Output cycle
Computers never get tired and are very fast
First generation used vacuum tubes; now we use microchips
comp_1.tip: The physical parts of a computer are called Hardware; the programs are Software.
comp_1.question: Who is known as the Father of Computing?

examples.question: Describe the Input-Process-Output (IPO) cycle for solving a math problem on a calculator.
steps: Input: You type numbers and signs like 5 + 3.
Process: The CPU calculates the sum internally.
Output: The number 8 appears on the screen.

examples.question: Why is a computer called a "fast" machine?
steps: It can perform millions of calculations in one second.
Humans take minutes to solve complex math; computers do it instantly.

scenarios.q: You type "2+2" into your calculator. What part of the IPO cycle is this?
scenarios.a: This is Input.

scenarios.q: You are waiting for a video to load on your tablet. What is the computer doing right now?
scenarios.a: It is Processing data from the internet.

scenarios.q: Your printed homework comes out of the machine. What part of the cycle is this?
scenarios.a: This is Output.

scenarios.q: A computer never gets "bored" of doing the same math 1000 times. What characteristic is this?
scenarios.a: Diligence (Computer doesn't get tired or bored).

scenarios.q: You are playing a heavy 3D game. Why does the computer get warm?
scenarios.a: The CPU is working very hard (Processing) and generating heat.

scenarios.q: If you turn off a computer while you are typing but before you save, where was that text stored?
scenarios.a: It was in the RAM (Temporary memory).

scenarios.q: You want to keep your photos forever. Where should you store them?
scenarios.a: On a Hard Disk or SSD (Permanent storage).

scenarios.q: Why is a computer considered "Garbage In, Garbage Out"?
scenarios.a: Because if you give wrong input, the computer will give wrong output.

scenarios.q: You are using a mouse to click a button. Is the mouse hardware or software?
scenarios.a: Hardware.

scenarios.q: The game you are playing is "Subway Surfers". Is this hardware or software?
scenarios.a: Software.

comp_2.what: Input devices give data to the computer (keyboard); Output devices show the results (monitor).
comp_2.example: A scanner takes a photo and puts it in the computer; a printer takes it out on paper.
points: Input: Keyboard, Mouse, Scanner, Microphone
Output: Monitor, Printer, Speakers, Projector
A touchscreen is both an input and an output device
comp_2.tip: The most common keyboard layout is called QWERTY!
comp_2.question: Is a printer an input or output device?

examples.question: If you want to put your paper photograph inside the computer, which device will you use?
steps: You will use a Scanner.
Place photo on the glass, press Scan, and it becomes a digital file.
examples.illustration: Scanner taking photo into the binary world

examples.question: Identify the output device needed to hear music.
steps: Speakers or Headphones.
They take digital signals and turn them into sound waves you can hear.

comp_3.what: The brain of the computer (CPU) and its storage (RAM/Hard Disk) work together to perform tasks.
comp_3.example: Think of the Hard Disk as a big cabinet and RAM as your desktop items you are using right now.
points: CPU (Central Processing Unit) does all the calculations
RAM is temporary memory; Hard Disk is permanent storage
The Motherboard connects all parts together
comp_3.tip: More RAM usually makes your computer faster when multi-tasking!
comp_3.question: What does CPU stand for?

examples.question: Why is RAM called "Volatile" memory?
steps: RAM holds data only as long as the computer is ON.
If you turn off the computer without saving, data in RAM is deleted.
That's why we need Hard Disks for permanent storage.
examples.illustration: RAM = Temporary workbench; Disk = Permanent cupboard

examples.question: What is the function of the Motherboard?
steps: It is the main circuit board that connects CPU, RAM, and all other parts.
It acts like the nervous system of the computer.

comp_os_1.what: The Operating System (like Windows) is the main software that manages everything on the computer.
comp_os_1.example: Android is the operating system for many mobile phones.
points: The Desktop is your main workspace on the screen
Icons are small pictures that represent programs
The Start Menu helps you find settings and apps
comp_os_1.tip: You can change your desktop background (wallpaper) to make it look unique!
comp_os_1.question: Name one popular operating system other than Windows.

comp_os_2.what: Files are individual items (like a document or photo); Folders are used to organize files.
comp_os_2.example: Think of a Folder as a drawer and Files as the papers inside it.
points: Creating a folder helps you group related work together
Renaming a file makes it easier to find later
Deleting an item puts it in the Recycle Bin (until you empty it)
comp_os_2.tip: Always give your folders descriptive names like "Science Projects"!
comp_os_2.question: Where do deleted files go on a Windows computer?

comp_word_1.what: MS Word / Google Docs are used to create beautiful documents with different fonts and formats.
comp_word_1.example: You can write a letter to your friend and make the text bold or blue.
points: B stands for Bold, I for Italic, and U for Underline
Alignment can be Left, Center, Right, or Justified
Font size makes text bigger or smaller
comp_word_1.tip: Shortcut: Ctrl + S saves your work instantly!
comp_word_1.question: Which button do you use to make text look slanting?

comp_word_2.what: Adding tables, images, and headers to make your documents professional.
comp_word_2.example: Inserting a colorful photo into your school report.
points: Tables are used to organize data into rows and columns
Headers appear at the top of every page
Page Layout helps you set margins and orientation
comp_word_2.tip: Use "Insert Table" for things like class timetables!
comp_word_2.question: Where are Headers located on a page?

comp_paint_1.what: Using basic tools in MS Paint to draw and fill shapes with colors.
comp_paint_1.example: Drawing a house using rectangles and a triangle for the roof.
points: Pencil tool is for freehand drawing
Fill with Color tool (bucket) fills a closed shape
Select tool allows you to move parts of your drawing
comp_paint_1.tip: If you make a mistake, use Ctrl + Z to Undo!
comp_paint_1.question: Which tool is used to fill a whole shape with a single color?

comp_prog_1.what: Scratch is a visual coding language where you snap blocks together like LEGO to make stories and games.
comp_prog_1.example: Making a cat (Sprite) say "Hello!" when you click the green flag.
points: Sprites are the characters on the screen
Backdrops are the backgrounds
The Stage is where the action happens
comp_prog_1.tip: Every script in Scratch starts with an Event block (like "When Green Flag Clicked")!
comp_prog_1.question: What is the character in Scratch called?

examples.question: How do you make a cat sprite move 50 steps in Scratch?
steps: Drag the "Move 10 steps" block.
Change the number 10 to 50.
Click the block or add an event block above it.
examples.illustration: Move block with [50] in the center

examples.question: How to change the background of your project?
steps: Go to the "Backdrops" section on the right.
Click "Choose a Backdrop" and select a new image.

comp_prog_1.textbookContent: Introduction to Scratch, a block-based visual programming language. It explains Sprites (characters), Stage (background), and Scripts (code blocks that snap together).

qa.a: A character or object that can move and perform actions in Scratch.

qa.q: What is the Green Flag used for?
qa.a: To start the execution of the scripts.

scenarios.q: You want your cat to walk non-stop across the screen. Which block do you use?
scenarios.a: The "forever" loop block.

scenarios.q: You want the backdrop to change when the cat touches a wall. Which block helps?
scenarios.a: The "Sensing" block (if touching edge) combined with "Looks" (next backdrop).

comp_prog_2.what: Loops repeat actions, and Conditions (if-else) help characters make decisions.
comp_prog_2.example: Telling a sprite to "repeat 10 times" so it walks across the stage.
points: A Repeat block saves you from writing the same code again and again
If-Else blocks are used for things like: If touching edge, then turn back
Sensing blocks help sprites detect things around them
comp_prog_2.tip: Using loops makes your code shorter and smarter.
comp_prog_2.question: Which block would you use to repeat an action forever?

comp_net_1.what: The Internet is a global network of computers connected to share information.
comp_net_1.example: Browsing Wikipedia to learn about space is using the internet.
points: A Web Browser is an app used to view websites
A Search Engine (like Google) helps you find specific info
WWW stands for World Wide Web
comp_net_1.tip: Always be careful and ask an adult before visiting new websites!
comp_net_1.question: What is the full form of WWW?

comp_net_3.what: Important rules to stay safe and protect your information while playing or studying online.
comp_net_3.example: Never sharing your home address or password with strangers online.
points: Use strong passwords with numbers and symbols
Don't click on suspicious links or pop-ups
Cyberbullying is wrong — always tell a teacher or parent if it happens
comp_net_3.tip: Think before you post! Anything you share online might stay there forever.
comp_net_3.question: Should you share your login password with your friends?

examples.question: Identify if this password is secure: "123456"
steps: No, it is very weak and easy for hackers to guess.
A strong password should be like: "@StUdEnT#2026".

examples.question: What should you do if you receive a message from a stranger online?
steps: Do not reply or click any links.
Immediately tell your parents or a trusted adult.

scenarios.q: You receive an email saying you won 1 crore rupees and should click a link. What do you do?
scenarios.a: Ignore it and tell an adult; it is likely a scam or "Phishing" attempt.

scenarios.q: A stranger on a gaming app asks for your home address to send you a gift. What do you do?
scenarios.a: Never share personal details like address or school name with strangers online.

scenarios.q: Your friend wants to use "password123" for their account. Is this safe?
scenarios.a: No, it is very weak. Suggest a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols.

scenarios.q: You see someone posting mean comments about a classmate on a group chat. What is this called?
scenarios.a: Cyberbullying.

scenarios.q: You want to download a "free" movie from an unknown website. What is the risk?
scenarios.a: It could contain a Virus or Malware that could damage your computer.

scenarios.q: You are using a public computer at a library. What should you do before leaving?
scenarios.a: Log out of all accounts and clear the browsing history.

scenarios.q: Should you post your vacation photos on social media while you are still away?
scenarios.a: It is safer to wait until you return so people don't know your house is empty.

scenarios.q: An app asks for permission to access your camera and location. What should you check?
scenarios.a: Check if the app actually needs them (e.g., a calculator app shouldn't need your location).

scenarios.q: You accidentally clicked on a pop-up that redirected you to a strange site. What is the first thing to do?
scenarios.a: Close the browser tab immediately and run a virus scan if possible.

scenarios.q: Why should you use "Private" or "Incognito" mode sometimes?
scenarios.a: So the computer doesn't save your search history or cookies on that session.

gk_ca_1.what: Staying updated with the latest news about India's achievements in sports, space, and science.
gk_ca_1.example: Knowing about the latest ISRO mission or a gold medal won at the Olympics.
points: India has made great progress in digital technology (UPI)
The Khelo India program encourages young sports stars
ISRO is one of the top space agencies in the world
gk_ca_1.tip: Reading a newspaper for 10 minutes daily is a great habit!
gk_ca_1.question: Which space agency of India recently sent missions to the Moon?

gk_ca_2.what: Understanding how the world works together through organizations like the UN and WHO.
gk_ca_2.example: The World Health Organization (WHO) helped countries during the pandemic.
points: The UN (United Nations) works for world peace
Climate change is a global issue that all countries are fighting together
Sustainable Development Goals aim to end poverty by 2030
gk_ca_2.tip: Knowledge of international events helps you become a global citizen.
gk_ca_2.question: What does WHO stand for?

gk_ind_1.what: Quick facts about our motherland — its states, beautiful symbols, and unique identity.
gk_ind_1.example: Did you know the Peacock is our national bird and the Banyan is our national tree?
points: India has 28 states and 8 union territories
The Tiger is our national animal
Our national anthem "Jana Gana Mana" was written by Rabindranath Tagore
gk_ind_1.tip: Each state in India has its own unique language and festival!
gk_ind_1.question: Which is the national tree of India?

examples.question: Identify the colors in the Indian National Flag and their meanings.
steps: Saffron: Courage and Sacrifice.
White: Peace and Truth.
Green: Fertility and Growth.
The wheel (Chakra): Progress.

examples.question: Who wrote our national song "Vande Mataram"?
steps: It was written by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay in his book "Anandamath".

gk_ind_2.what: Fascinating facts about the world's continents, oceans, and extreme geographical features.
gk_ind_2.example: Mt. Everest is the highest point on Earth, located in the Himalayas.
points: Asia is the largest continent; Australia is the smallest
The Pacific Ocean is so deep that Mt. Everest could fit inside it!
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world
gk_ind_2.tip: There are seven continents and five major oceans on our planet.
gk_ind_2.question: Which is the smallest continent in the world?

gk_ind_3.what: Inspiring stories of people who changed the world through their bravery, science, or art.
gk_ind_3.example: Mahatma Gandhi led the struggle for India's independence through non-violence.
points: APJ Abdul Kalam was the "Missile Man of India"
Mother Teresa was known for her selfless service to the poor
Sachin Tendulkar is one of the greatest cricketers of all time
gk_ind_3.tip: Read biographies of great people to get motivated!
gk_ind_3.question: Who is known as the "Missile Man of India"?

gk_sci_1.what: Learning about the inventions that made our lives easier, from the lightbulb to the internet.
gk_sci_1.example: Thomas Edison spent years testing thousands of materials to make the first lightbulb.
points: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone
The Wright brothers made the first successful flight in an airplane
Charles Babbage is credited with the idea of the computer
gk_sci_1.tip: An invention starts with a simple "Why not?" or "What if?"
gk_sci_1.question: Who invented the lightbulb?

examples.question: How did the invention of the steam engine change history?
steps: It allowed trains and ships to travel much faster without needing wind or horses.
It led to the growth of factories and modern industries.

examples.question: Identify the inventor of the Computer.
steps: Charles Babbage is known as the inventor, though Ada Lovelace wrote the first program for it.

gk_sci_2.what: Basic understanding of how our amazing body works — our heart, brain, and lungs.
gk_sci_2.example: Your heart pumps blood to every part of your body without ever stopping!
points: The brain is the control center for everything you do
Lungs help you take in oxygen and get rid of carbon dioxide
Stomach and intestines help turn food into energy
gk_sci_2.tip: Your body is the most complex machine in the world — take good care of it!
gk_sci_2.question: Which organ in our body is responsible for thinking?

eng_gr_1.what: Exploring Nouns (names of things/people) and Pronouns (words used instead of names).
eng_gr_1.example: Instead of saying "Rahul is good", we say "He is good". "He" is a pronoun.
points: Nouns can be Common (dog) or Proper (Fido)
Collective nouns describe groups (a flock of birds)
Pronouns like I, you, we, they make sentences smoother
eng_gr_1.tip: Proper nouns always start with a capital letter!
eng_gr_1.question: Can you find three nouns and two pronouns in your room?

examples.question: Identify nouns in: "Sadiya drove her car to the park in Bangalore."
steps: Names of people: Sadiya.
Things: car, park.
Places: Bangalore.
All these are nouns.

examples.question: Replace the noun with a pronoun: "The boy is eating a sandwich because the boy is hungry."
steps: Identify the repeated noun: "the boy".
Use "He" for the second part.
Result: "The boy is eating a sandwich because he is hungry."

scenarios.q: You are introducing your friends, Rahul and Priya, to your teacher. What nouns are you using?
scenarios.a: Rahul and Priya are Proper Nouns; "Friends" and "Teacher" are Common Nouns.

scenarios.q: Instead of repeatedly saying "The dog is big. The dog is brown. The dog is playing.", how can you use a pronoun?
scenarios.a: Use "It": "The dog is big. It is brown and it is playing."

scenarios.q: You see a group of birds flying together. What collective noun can you use?
scenarios.a: A "flock" of birds.

scenarios.q: Your sister's name is Ananya. If you are talking to her, which pronoun do you use?
scenarios.a: You use "You". If talking *about* her, you use "She".

scenarios.q: You found a bunch of keys on the floor. Identify the noun and its type.
scenarios.a: "Keys" is a common noun; "Bunch" is a collective noun.

scenarios.q: You are writing about yourself. Which pronoun is always used?
scenarios.a: The pronoun "I".

scenarios.q: Someone left "their" bag in the classroom. What kind of pronoun is "their"?
scenarios.a: A possessive pronoun, showing who the bag belongs to.

scenarios.q: You want to name a specific ocean. What kind of noun is "Indian Ocean"?
scenarios.a: A Proper Noun.

scenarios.q: A cat is chasing "its" tail. Why did we use "its" instead of "his" or "her"?
scenarios.a: We use "its" for animals when the gender is not specific or important.

scenarios.q: You feel a "happiness" when you see your gift. What type of noun is happiness?
scenarios.a: An Abstract Noun, because you can feel it but not touch it.

eng_gr_2.what: Verbs are action words that tell us what someone or something is doing.
eng_gr_2.example: Run, jump, study, and even "is" are all verbs.
points: Main verbs show the primary action
Helping verbs like "am, is, are" support the main verb
Every complete sentence must have atleast one verb
eng_gr_2.tip: Verbs are the engine of a sentence — they keep it moving!
eng_gr_2.question: What is the verb in the sentence: "Sadiya is reading a book"?

examples.question: Find the verb in: "The birds are flying southward for winter."
steps: What are the birds doing? They "are flying".
"Flying" is the main action verb.

examples.question: Identify the helping verb in: "I have finished my homework."
steps: Main action is "finished".
"Have" is helping the main action.

eng_gr_3.what: Adjectives describe Nouns, while Adverbs describe Verbs, Adjectives, or other Adverbs.
points: Adjectives add color and detail to our descriptions
Adverbs often end in "-ly" (slowly, carefully)
They help make our writing more interesting and vivid
eng_gr_3.tip: Adverbs answer the question: How, When, or Where?
eng_gr_3.question: In "The happy girl sang loudly", which word is the adverb?

examples.question: Add an adjective and an adverb to this: "The dog barked."
steps: Adjective describes dog: "The *angry* dog".
Adverb describes barking: "barked *loudly*".
Result: "The angry dog barked loudly."

examples.question: Find the adverb in: "She solved the difficult puzzle easily."
steps: How did she solve it? "Easily".
"Easily" is the adverb.

eng_gr_4.what: Tenses show when an action happens — in the past, present, or future.
eng_gr_4.example: I ate (past), I eat (present), I will eat (future).
points: Present tense is for things happening now or habits
Past tense describes what is already finished
Future tense tells us what is going to happen
eng_gr_4.tip: Most past tense verbs end in "-ed", but some are irregular like "ran"!
eng_gr_4.question: Change "I walk to school" into the future tense.

examples.question: Change "They sing a song" into Past Tense.
steps: Identify verb: "sing".
Past form of sing is "sang".
Result: "They sang a song."

examples.question: Write "We play cricket" in Future Tense.
steps: Add "will" before the verb.
Result: "We will play cricket."

eng_gr_5.what: Articles (a, an, the) and Prepositions (in, on, at) are small words with big roles.
points: Use "an" before words starting with vowel sounds (a,e,i,o,u)
Prepositions show the position or time of something
"The" is used for specific things we already know about
eng_gr_5.tip: Don't say "a onion", say "an onion" because 'o' is a vowel!
eng_gr_5.question: Which preposition would you use to say where your pencil is?

examples.question: Fill in the blanks: "___ elephant is standing ___ the tree."
steps: Elephant starts with vowel sound (e), so use "An".
Standing next to or under? Use "under".
Result: "An elephant is standing under the tree."

examples.question: Explain use of "a" vs "an" with "university" and "hour".
steps: University starts with "y" sound (consonant) -> Use "a university".
Hour starts with "o" sound (vowel) -> Use "an hour".

eng_gr_6.what: A sentence is a group of words that makes complete sense, starting with a capital and ending with a punctuation.
eng_gr_6.example: "The blue sky" is a phrase. "The sky is blue." is a sentence.
points: Every sentence has a Subject (who) and a Predicate (what they do)
Declarative sentences make statements
Interrogative sentences ask questions (?)
eng_gr_6.tip: A sentence is like a train — it needs an engine (verb) to go!
eng_gr_6.question: Is "Eating an apple" a complete sentence?

examples.question: Split this sentence into subject and predicate: "The large cat slept on the mat."
steps: Who is it about? "The large cat" (Subject).
What happened? "slept on the mat" (Predicate).

examples.question: Identify the type of sentence: "Please close the door."
steps: It gives an order or request.
Therefore, it is an Imperative sentence.

eng_gr_7.what: Active voice means the subject does the action. Passive voice means the action is done to the subject.
eng_gr_7.example: Active: Sadiya wrote a story. Passive: A story was written by Sadiya.
points: Active voice is direct and stronger
Passive voice uses the verb "to be" and a past participle
Use passive voice when the result is more important than the doer
eng_gr_7.tip: If it was done by someone, start with them for Active voice!
eng_gr_7.question: Is "The window was broken" active or passive?

examples.question: Change to Passive: "Rahul painted the fence."
steps: Object "the fence" comes first.
Add "was painted by".
Result: "The fence was painted by Rahul."

examples.question: Change to Active: "The cake was eaten by the dog."
steps: The dog (doer) comes first.
Result: "The dog ate the cake."

eng_gr_10.what: Story writing is the art of creating a sequence of events with characters, a setting, and a plot.
eng_gr_10.example: Every great story starts with a simple "Once upon a time..."
points: Introduce your characters and where they are
Give them a problem to solve (the plot)
Show how they solve it and what happens in the end
eng_gr_10.tip: Show, don't just tell! Instead of "he was sad", describe his tears.
eng_gr_10.question: What are the three main parts of any good story?

examples.question: Suggest a title and a moral for a story about an honest woodcutter.
steps: Title: "The Honest Woodcutter".
Moral: "Honesty is the best policy."

examples.question: Write a beginning sentence for a story about a dragon.
steps: "Far beyond the misty mountains, in a cave made of blue ice, lived a dragon who was afraid of fire."

hin_gr_2.what: क्रिया किसी काम के होने या करने का बोध कराती है। विशेषण संज्ञा या सर्वनाम की विशेषता बताते हैं।
points: क्रिया के बिना वाक्य अधूरा होता है
विशेषण शब्द रंग, आकार, गुण आदि बताते हैं
जैसे: मोटा, सुंदर, काला, चार
hin_gr_2.tip: विशेषण हमेशा संज्ञा से पहले या बाद में आकर उसकी खूबी बताता है।
hin_gr_2.question: "नीला आकाश" में विशेषण शब्द कौन सा है?

hin_gr_3.what: शब्दों के सार्थक समूह को वाक्य कहते हैं। वाक्य के मुख्य दो भाग होते हैं: उद्देश्य और विधेय।
points: वाक्य से मन के भाव स्पष्ट होते हैं
सार्थक शब्दों का सही क्रम होना आवश्यक है
जैसे: "है रहा पढ़ राम" गलत है, "राम पढ़ रहा है" सही है
hin_gr_3.tip: वाक्य के अंत में पूर्ण विराम (।) लगाना न भूलें!
hin_gr_3.question: वाक्य किसे कहते हैं?

hin_ram_3.what: रानी कैकेयी द्वारा राजा दशरथ से दो वरदान माँगने और राम को १४ वर्ष का वनवास मिलने की कहानी।
hin_ram_3.example: कैकेयी ने मंथरा की बातों में आकर राम के लिए वनवास माँगा।
points: पहला वरदान: भरत का राजतिलक
दूसरा वरदान: राम को १४ वर्ष का वनवास
राजा दशरथ इस निर्णय से बहुत दुखी थे
hin_ram_3.tip: बुरी संगति हमें गलत निर्णय लेने पर मजबूर कर सकती है।
hin_ram_3.question: कैकेयी को भड़काने वाली दासी का क्या नाम था?

hin_ram_4.what: राम, सीता और लक्ष्मण का अयोध्या त्यागकर वन की ओर प्रस्थान करने का मार्मिक वर्णन।
hin_ram_4.example: पूरी अयोध्या नगरी राम के पीछे वन जाने के लिए तैयार थी।
points: राम ने प्रसन्नतापूर्वक पिता की आज्ञा का पालन किया
सीता और लक्ष्मण ने भी राम के साथ जाने का निश्चय किया
केवट ने उन्हें अपनी नाव से गंगा पार कराई
hin_ram_4.tip: पितृ-भक्ति और कर्तव्यपरायणता का उत्तम उदाहरण!
hin_ram_4.question: गंगा पार कराते समय राम की मदद किसने की?

comp_prog_3.what: Blockly is another visual programming tool that helps you learn the logic of coding by stacking colorful blocks.
comp_prog_3.example: Dragging a "Move Forward" block to make a character reach its goal.
points: It is very similar to Scratch but used in many different apps
Helps in understanding how step-by-step instructions (algorithms) work
No need to worry about typing errors or syntax!
comp_prog_3.tip: Coding is just a way of talking to a computer in its own language.
comp_prog_3.question: Why is visual coding easier for beginners?

comp_net_2.what: Email (Electronic Mail) is a way to send messages and files instantly across the world using the internet.
comp_net_2.example: Sending a greeting card to your cousin in another country via Gmail.
points: Every user needs a unique Email ID (like student@school.com)
The "Subject" line tells what the email is about
The "Compose" button is used to start a new email
comp_net_2.tip: Always check for spelling mistakes before clicking "Send"!
comp_net_2.question: What does "Subject" mean in an email?

kan_lit_1.what: ಕನ್ನಡ ನಾಡು ನುಡಿ ಎಂದರೆ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ಶ್ರೀಮಂತ ಸಾಂಸ್ಕೃತಿಕ ಪರಂಪರೆ ಮತ್ತು ಕನ್ನಡ ಭಾಷೆಯ ಉಗಮದ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ತಿಳಿಯುವುದು.
kan_lit_1.example: ಕನ್ನಡ ಭಾಷೆಯು ೨೦೦೦ ವರ್ಷಗಳಿಗಿಂತಲೂ ಹೆಚ್ಚು ಇತಿಹಾಸವನ್ನು ಹೊಂದಿದೆ ಮತ್ತು ಇದು ಎಂಟು ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪ್ರಶಸ್ತಿಗಳನ್ನು ಪಡೆದಿದೆ.
points: ಕುವೆಂಪು, ದ.ರಾ. ಬೇಂದ್ರೆ ರಂತಹ ಮಹಾನ್ ಕವಿಗಳು ಸಾಹಿತ್ಯಕ್ಕೆ ಸೇವೆ ಸಲ್ಲಿಸಿದ್ದಾರೆ.
ಕರ್ನಾಟಕವು ತನ್ನದೇ ಆದ ವಿಶಿಷ್ಟ ಕಲೆ ಮತ್ತು ಸಂಸ್ಕೃತಿಯನ್ನು ಹೊಂದಿದೆ.
kan_lit_1.tip: ಪ್ರತಿದಿನ ಕನ್ನಡ ಸಾಹಿತ್ಯದ ಒಂದು ಸಣ್ಣ ಭಾಗವನ್ನು ಓದುವ ಅಭ್ಯಾಸ ಮಾಡಿಕೊಳ್ಳಿ!
kan_lit_1.question: ಮೊದಲ ಕನ್ನಡ ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪ್ರಶಸ್ತಿ ಪಡೆದವರು ಯಾರು?

examples.question: ಕನ್ನಡದ ಮೊದಲ ಗ್ರಂಥ ಯಾವುದು?
steps: ಕನ್ನಡದ ಮೊದಲ ಉಪಲಬ್ಧ ಗ್ರಂಥವೆಂದರೆ "ಕವಿರಾಜಮಾರ್ಗ".
ಇದನ್ನು ಅಮೋಘವರ್ಷ ನೃಪತುಂಗನು ಬರೆಸಿದನು.

examples.question: ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ರಾಜ್ಯ ಲಾಂಛನ ಯಾವುದು?
steps: ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ರಾಜ್ಯ ಲಾಂಛನ "ಗಂಡಭೇರುಂಡ".
ಇದು ಎರಡು ತಲೆಗಳನ್ನು ಹೊಂದಿರುವ ಕಾಲ್ಪನಿಕ ಪಕ್ಷಿಯಾಗಿದೆ.

kan_lit_1.textbookContent: ಸಿರಿ ಕನ್ನಡ ಪಠ್ಯಪುಸ್ತಕದ ಮೊದಲ ಪಾಠ "ಕನ್ನಡ ನಾಡು ನುಡಿ" ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ಇತಿಹಾಸ ಮತ್ತು ಸಾಹಿತ್ಯ ಸೌರಭವನ್ನು ಪರಿಚಯಿಸುತ್ತದೆ. ಇದು ಕನ್ನಡ ಕವಿಗಳ ಕೊಡುಗೆ ಮತ್ತು ಭಾಷೆಯ ಪ್ರಾಚೀನತೆಯನ್ನು ವಿವರಿಸುತ್ತದೆ.

qa.q: ಕನ್ನಡ ಅತಿ ಪ್ರಾಚೀನ ಭಾಷೆ ಎಂದು ಹೇಗೆ ಹೇಳುತ್ತಾರೆ?
qa.a: ಕ್ರಿ.ಶ. ೪೫೦ರ ಹಲ್ಮಿಡಿ ಶಾಸನವು ಕನ್ನಡದ ಪ್ರಾಚೀನತೆಗೆ ಸಾಕ್ಷಿಯಾಗಿದೆ.

qa.q: ಕನ್ನಡದ ಮೊದಲ ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪ್ರಶಸ್ತಿ ವಿಜೇತ ಯಾರು?
qa.a: ಕುವೆಂಪು (ಶ್ರೀ ರಾಮಾಯಣ ದರ್ಶನಂ ಕೃತಿಗೆ).
"ಜಯ ಭಾರತ ಜನನಿಯ ತನುಜಾತೆ".
ಬೆಂಗಳೂರಿನಲ್ಲಿದೆ.
ಪಂಪ ಕವಿಯನ್ನು.
ಮಿತ್ರವಿಂದಾ ಗೋವಿಂದ.
ಬಸವಣ್ಣನವರು.
ನವೆಂಬರ್ ೧.
ಹಲ್ಮಿಡಿ ಶಾಸನ.

qa.q: ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪ್ರಶಸ್ತಿ ಪಡೆದ ಮೊದಲ ಮಹಿಳೆ ಯಾರು?
qa.a: ಕನ್ನಡದಲ್ಲಿ ಇನ್ನೂ ಮಹಿಳೆಯರು ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪಡೆದಿಲ್ಲ (Ashapurna Devi was first in India).

scenarios.q: ನೀವು ಹಲ್ಮಿಡಿ ಗ್ರಾಮಕ್ಕೆ ಭೇಟಿ ನೀಡಿದ್ದೀರಿ. ಅಲ್ಲಿ ನೀವು ನೋಡುವ ಅತಿ ಪ್ರಮುಖ ಐತಿಹಾಸಿಕ ವಸ್ತು ಯಾವುದು?
scenarios.a: ಹಲ್ಮಿಡಿ ಶಾಸನ (ಕನ್ನಡದ ಮೊದಲ ಶಾಸನ).

scenarios.q: ಒಬ್ಬ ಬಾಲಕ ಕುವೆಂಪು ಅವರ ಮನೆಗೆ ಹೋಗಿದ್ದಾನೆ. ಅವನು ನೋಡಬಹುದಾದ ಪ್ರಮುಖ ವಸ್ತು ಯಾವುದು?
scenarios.a: ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪ್ರಶಸ್ತಿ ಫಲಕ.

scenarios.q: ಒಬ್ಬ ಪ್ರವಾಸಿ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕಕ್ಕೆ ಬಂದಾಗ ಮೊದಲು ಯಾವ ಭಾಷೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಮಾತನಾಡಲು ಪ್ರಯತ್ನಿಸಬೇಕು?
scenarios.a: ಕನ್ನಡದಲ್ಲಿ.

scenarios.q: ಶಾಲೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಕನ್ನಡ ರಾಜ್ಯೋತ್ಸವ ಆಚರಿಸುವಾಗ ಯಾವ ಹಾಡು ಕಡ್ಡಾಯ?
scenarios.a: ಜಯ ಭಾರತ ಜನನಿಯ ತನುಜಾತೆ.

scenarios.q: ಒಬ್ಬ ಕವಿ ಬೆಟ್ಟದ ಮೇಲೆ ಕುಳಿತು ಪ್ರಕೃತಿಯ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಬರೆಯುತ್ತಾನೆ. ಇದು ಯಾವ ತರಹದ ಸಾಹಿತ್ಯ?
scenarios.a: ಭಾವಗೀತೆ ಅಥವಾ ನಿಸರ್ಗ ಕವನ.

scenarios.q: ನೀವು ಹಳೆಯ ಓಲೆಗರಿಗಳನ್ನು ನೋಡಿದಾಗ ಅದನ್ನು ಏನನ್ನುತ್ತೀರಿ?
scenarios.a: ಹಸ್ತಪ್ರತಿ ಅಥವಾ ತಾಳೆಗರಿ.

scenarios.q: ಒಬ್ಬ ವಿದೇಶಿ ಗೆಳೆಯನಿಗೆ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ಸಂಸ್ಕೃತಿಯ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ತಿಳಿಸಲು ನೀವು ಯಾವ ಕಲೆಯ ಬಗ್ಗೆ ಹೇಳುತ್ತೀರಿ?
scenarios.a: ಯಕ್ಷಗಾನ ಅಥವಾ ವೀರಗಾಸೆ.

scenarios.q: ಕನ್ನಡ ಅಕ್ಷರಮಾಲೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ೪೯ ಅಕ್ಷರಗಳಿವೆ ಎಂದು ನಿಮ್ಮ ಗೆಳೆಯನಿಗೆ ಹೇಗೆ ವಿವರಿಸುತ್ತೀರಿ?
scenarios.a: ಸ್ವರಗಳು, ವ್ಯಂಜನಗಳು ಮತ್ತು ಯೋಗವಾಹಗಳ ಮೊತ್ತವೇ ೪೯.

scenarios.q: ವಚನಗಳನ್ನು ಓದುವಾಗ ನೀವು ಗಮನಿಸುವ ಮುಖ್ಯ ಅಂಶ ಯಾವುದು?
scenarios.a: ಸರಳ ಭಾಷೆ ಮತ್ತು ಸಮಾಜದ ಸುಧಾರಣೆಯ ಸಂದೇಶ.

scenarios.q: ಜ್ಞಾನಪೀಠ ಪ್ರಶಸ್ತಿ ವಿಜೇತರ ಸಾಲಿನಲ್ಲಿ ನೀವು ಯಾರ ಹೆಸರನ್ನು ಮೊದಲು ನೆನಪಿಸಿಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತೀರಿ?
scenarios.a: ಕುವೆಂಪು ಅಥವಾ ದ.ರಾ. ಬೇಂದ್ರೆ.

kan_lit_2.what: ಗಿಡದ ಗಾಳಿ ಪದ್ಯವು ಗಿಡಮರಗಳ ಮಹತ್ವ ಮತ್ತು ಅವು ನಮಗೆ ಹೇಗೆ ಉಸಿರನ್ನು ನೀಡುತ್ತವೆ ಎಂಬುದನ್ನು ವಿವರಿಸುತ್ತದೆ.
kan_lit_2.example: ಗಿಡವು ಹೇಗೆ ತನ್ನ ಕಂಪು ಮತ್ತು ಹಸಿರಿನಿಂದ ಎಲ್ಲರ ಮನಸ್ಸನ್ನು ಸಂತೋಷಪಡಿಸುತ್ತದೆ ಎಂಬುದು ಈ ಪದ್ಯದ ಸಾರ.
points: ಗಿಡಗಳು ನಮಗೆ ನೆರಳು ಮತ್ತು ಹಣ್ಣುಗಳನ್ನು ನೀಡುತ್ತವೆ.
ನೈಸರ್ಗಿಕ ಸಮತೋಲನವನ್ನು ಕಾಪಾಡಲು ಗಿಡಗಳನ್ನು ಬೆಳೆಸಬೇಕು.
kan_lit_2.question: ಹಸಿರೇ ಉಸಿರು ಎಂದರೆ ಏನು?

examples.question: ಗಿಡಗಳು ನಮಗೆ ಯಾವ ಅನಿಲವನ್ನು ನೀಡುತ್ತವೆ?
steps: ಗಿಡಗಳು ದ್ಯುತಿಸಂಶ್ಲೇಷಣೆಯ ಮೂಲಕ ನಮಗೆ "ಆಮ್ಲಜನಕ"ವನ್ನು (Oxygen) ನೀಡುತ್ತವೆ.

examples.question: ಮರಗಳನ್ನು ಕಡಿಯುವುದರಿಂದ ಆಗುವ ಅನಾನುಕೂಲತೆಗಳೇನು?
steps: ಮಳೆ ಕಡಿಮೆಯಾಗುತ್ತದೆ ಮತ್ತು ಜಾಗತಿಕ ತಾಪಮಾನ ಹೆಚ್ಚಾಗುತ್ತದೆ.

kan_gr_1.what: ಕನ್ನಡ ಅಕ್ಷರಮಾಲೆಯು ಭಾಷೆಯ ಬುನಾದಿ. ಇದರಲ್ಲಿ ಒಟ್ಟು ೪೯ ಅಕ್ಷರಗಳಿವೆ.
kan_gr_1.example: ಸ್ವರಗಳು (ಅ, ಆ...), ವ್ಯಂಜನಗಳು (ಕ, ಖ...) ಮತ್ತು ಯೋಗವಾಹಗಳು (ಅಂ, ಅಃ) ಎಂಬ ಮೂರು ವಿಧಗಳಿವೆ.
points: ಸ್ವರಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಹ್ರಸ್ವ ಸ್ವರ ಮತ್ತು ದೀರ್ಘ ಸ್ವರ ಎಂಬ ವಿಧಗಳಿವೆ.
ಯೋಗವಾಹಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಅನುಸ್ವಾರ ಮತ್ತು ವಿಸರ್ಗ ಎಂಬ ಎರಡು ವಿಧಗಳಿವೆ.
kan_gr_1.tip: ಅಕ್ಷರಗಳನ್ನು ಸ್ಪಷ್ಟವಾಗಿ ಉಚ್ಚರಿಸುವುದು ಭಾಷೆಯ ಕಲಿಕೆಗೆ ಅತಿ ಮುಖ್ಯ!
kan_gr_1.question: ಕನ್ನಡ ವರ್ಣಮಾಲೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಎಷ್ಟು ಸ್ವರಗಳಿವೆ?

examples.question: ಹ್ರಸ್ವ ಸ್ವರ ಮತ್ತು ದೀರ್ಘ ಸ್ವರಕ್ಕೆ ಉದಾಹರಣೆ ಕೊಡಿ.
steps: ಹ್ರಸ್ವ ಸ್ವರ: ಅ, ಇ, ಉ.
ದೀರ್ಘ ಸ್ವರ: ಆ, ಈ, ಊ.

examples.question: ಯೋಗವಾಹಗಳು ಯಾವುವು?
steps: "ಅಂ" (ಅನುಸ್ವಾರ) ಮತ್ತು "ಅಃ" (ವಿಸರ್ಗ) ಇವುಗಳು ಯೋಗವಾಹಗಳು.

kan_gr_2.what: ನಾಮಪದವು ಯಾವುದೇ ವಸ್ತು, ವ್ಯಕ್ತಿ ಅಥವಾ ಸ್ಥಳದ ಹೆಸರನ್ನು ಸೂಚಿಸುವ ಪದವಾಗಿದೆ.
kan_gr_2.example: ರಾಮು ಶಾಲೆಗೆ ಹೋದನು. ಇಲ್ಲಿ "ರಾಮು" ಮತ್ತು "ಶಾಲೆ" ಎಂಬವು ನಾಮಪದಗಳು.
points: ನಾಮಪದದಲ್ಲಿ ಅಂಕಿತನಾಮ, ರೂಢನಾಮ ಮತ್ತು ಅನ್ವರ್ಥನಾಮ ಎಂಬ ವಿಧಗಳಿವೆ.
ವಿಭಕ್ತಿ ಪ್ರತ್ಯಯಗಳು ವಾಕ್ಯದ ಅರ್ಥವನ್ನು ಪೂರ್ಣಗೊಳಿಸಲು ಸಹಾಯ ಮಾಡುತ್ತವೆ.
kan_gr_2.tip: ವಾಕ್ಯದಲ್ಲಿನ ಕಮಲ, ಗುಲಾಬಿ, ಬೆಂಗಳೂರು ಇವುಗಳನ್ನು ನಾಮಪದಗಳೆಂದು ಗುರುತಿಸಿ!
kan_gr_2.question: ಬೆಂಗಳೂರು ಇದು ಯಾವ ರೀತಿಯ ನಾಮಪದ?

examples.question: ಅಂಕಿತನಾಮ ಮತ್ತು ರೂಢನಾಮದ ವ್ಯತ್ಯಾಸವೇನು?
steps: ಅಂಕಿತನಾಮ: ನಿರ್ದಿಷ್ಟ ಹೆಸರು (ಉದಾ: ಮೈಸೂರು).
ರೂಢನಾಮ: ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯ ಹೆಸರು (ಉದಾ: ನಗರ).

examples.question: ಪ್ರಥಮಾ ವಿಭಕ್ತಿಯ ಪ್ರತ್ಯಯ ಯಾವುದು?
steps: ಪ್ರಥಮಾ ವಿಭಕ್ತಿಯ ಪ್ರತ್ಯಯ "ಉ". ಉದಾ: ರಾಮನು (ರಾಮ + ಉ).

eng_sup_4.what: A kind shopkeeper helps a poor man fix his old clock, and in return receives an unexpected reward.
eng_sup_4.example: Have you ever helped someone without expecting anything in return?
points: The shopkeeper repairs the clock for free out of kindness
The old man turns out to be a wealthy person in disguise
Acts of generosity often bring unexpected blessings
eng_sup_4.tip: Kindness is never wasted — it always comes back in some form!
eng_sup_4.question: Why did the shopkeeper help the old man?
tryIt: Think of one act of kindness you can do today for someone in need.
Write a short paragraph about how helping others makes you feel.
Share a story with your family about a time someone helped you.
thinkDiscuss: Do you think the shopkeeper would have helped if he knew the man was wealthy?
What does this story teach us about judging people by their appearance?

eng_sup_5.what: Tansen was the greatest musician in Emperor Akbar's court, famous for his magical voice that could light lamps and bring rain.
eng_sup_5.example: Imagine singing so beautifully that nature itself responds to your voice!
points: Tansen learned music from the great guru Swami Haridas
His Raga Deepak could light lamps, and Raga Megh Malhar brought rain
He was one of the Navaratnas (nine jewels) in Akbar's court
eng_sup_5.tip: True mastery comes from years of dedicated practice and learning from great teachers!
eng_sup_5.question: What made Tansen's music so special?
tryIt: Listen to a classical Indian raga and notice how it makes you feel.
Try humming a tune and see if you can make someone smile.
Research one other Navaratna from Akbar's court.
thinkDiscuss: Why do you think music has such a powerful effect on our emotions?
Can dedication and practice make anyone a master like Tansen?

eng_sup_6.what: A clever monkey outwits a crocodile who tries to trick him, teaching us about quick thinking and true friendship.
eng_sup_6.example: Have you ever had to think fast to get out of a tricky situation?
points: The monkey and crocodile become friends despite being different
The crocodile's wife wants to eat the monkey's heart
The monkey uses his intelligence to escape the crocodile's trap
eng_sup_6.tip: Quick thinking and presence of mind can save you from danger!
eng_sup_6.question: How did the monkey escape from the crocodile?
tryIt: Act out the story with friends, taking turns being the monkey and crocodile.
Write an alternate ending where the crocodile refuses to trick his friend.
Draw a comic strip showing the key moments of the story.
thinkDiscuss: Was the crocodile a true friend? Why or why not?
What would you do if someone asked you to betray a friend?

eng_sup_7.what: Sleep is a natural state of rest that our body and brain need to stay healthy, repair themselves, and process memories.
eng_sup_7.example: Just like your phone needs charging, your body needs sleep to recharge!
points: We spend about one-third of our lives sleeping
During sleep, our brain organizes memories and our body repairs itself
Dreams occur during REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep
eng_sup_7.tip: Getting 8-10 hours of sleep helps you learn better and stay healthy!
eng_sup_7.question: Why do we need sleep?

activity.title: Sleep Diary Experiment
instructions: For one week, note down what time you go to bed and wake up.
Rate how you feel each morning (1-5 scale: tired to energetic).
Notice if sleeping more or less affects your mood and energy.
Share your findings with the class.
activity.outcome: You'll discover your ideal sleep duration and how it affects your daily performance.

thinkDiscuss: What happens to your body and mind when you don't get enough sleep?
Why do babies sleep so much more than adults?

eng_sup_8.what: A brave girl makes a pact with the Sun to help her sick mother recover by bringing sunlight into their dark house.
eng_sup_8.example: Sometimes we need courage to ask for help, even from nature itself!
points: The girl's mother is sick because their house gets no sunlight
She bravely asks the Sun to shine into their home
The Sun agrees, and the mother recovers with warmth and light
eng_sup_8.tip: Sunlight and fresh air are essential for good health!
eng_sup_8.question: Why was the mother sick?

activity.title: Sunlight and Health
instructions: Place one plant in sunlight and another in a dark cupboard.
Water both plants equally for one week.
Observe and note the differences in their growth and color.
Discuss why sunlight is important for living things.
activity.outcome: The plant in sunlight will be healthier, showing how vital sunlight is for life.

thinkDiscuss: How does sunlight affect our mood and health?
What can we do if our house doesn't get enough natural light?

eng_gr_8.what: A paragraph is a group of sentences about one main idea, with a topic sentence, supporting details, and a concluding sentence.
eng_gr_8.example: Think of a paragraph like a sandwich: the topic sentence is the top bun, details are the filling, and the conclusion is the bottom bun!
points: Start with a clear topic sentence that states the main idea
Add 3-5 supporting sentences with details and examples
End with a concluding sentence that wraps up the idea
eng_gr_8.tip: Use transition words like 'firstly', 'moreover', 'finally' to connect your sentences smoothly!
eng_gr_8.question: What are the three parts of a good paragraph?
tryIt: Write a paragraph about your favorite hobby (5-6 sentences).
Underline the topic sentence, circle the supporting details, and highlight the conclusion.
Exchange paragraphs with a classmate and give feedback.

examples.question: Write a paragraph about 'My School'.
steps: Topic sentence: My school is a wonderful place to learn and grow.
Supporting details: It has spacious classrooms, a large playground, and a well-stocked library. The teachers are kind and helpful. We have many fun activities like sports and art.
Conclusion: I feel lucky to study in such a great school.

eng_gr_9.what: Letter writing is a way to communicate formally or informally through written messages, following specific formats.
eng_gr_9.example: Writing a letter is like having a conversation on paper with someone far away!
points: Formal letters: used for official purposes (applications, complaints)
Informal letters: used for personal communication (friends, family)
Both types have specific formats with sender's address, date, greeting, body, and closing
eng_gr_9.tip: Always proofread your letter before sending — first impressions matter!
eng_gr_9.question: What is the difference between formal and informal letters?
tryIt: Write an informal letter to a friend describing your recent vacation.
Write a formal letter to your principal requesting a library book.
Compare the tone and language used in both letters.

examples.question: Format of an informal letter
steps: Sender's address (top right)
Date
Greeting: Dear [Name],
Body: 2-3 paragraphs sharing news
Closing: Your friend, [Your name]

examples.question: Format of a formal letter
steps: Sender's address (top left)
Date
Receiver's address
Subject line
Salutation: Respected Sir/Madam,
Body: Clear, polite, to-the-point
Closing: Yours sincerely, [Your name]

hin_gr_4.what: पत्र लेखन औपचारिक या अनौपचारिक संदेश भेजने का एक तरीका है, जिसमें विशेष प्रारूप का पालन किया जाता है।
hin_gr_4.example: पत्र लिखना कागज़ पर किसी दूर के व्यक्ति से बातचीत करने जैसा है!
points: औपचारिक पत्र: आधिकारिक उद्देश्यों के लिए (आवेदन, शिकायत)
अनौपचारिक पत्र: व्यक्तिगत संचार के लिए (मित्र, परिवार)
दोनों प्रकार के पत्रों में विशेष प्रारूप होता है
hin_gr_4.tip: पत्र भेजने से पहले हमेशा जाँच लें — पहली छाप महत्वपूर्ण होती है!
hin_gr_4.question: औपचारिक और अनौपचारिक पत्र में क्या अंतर है?
tryIt: अपने मित्र को छुट्टियों के बारे में एक अनौपचारिक पत्र लिखें।
प्रधानाचार्य को पुस्तकालय की किताब के लिए औपचारिक पत्र लिखें।

examples.question: अनौपचारिक पत्र का प्रारूप
steps: प्रेषक का पता (ऊपर दाईं ओर)
दिनांक
अभिवादन: प्रिय [नाम],
मुख्य भाग: 2-3 अनुच्छेद
समापन: तुम्हारा मित्र, [तुम्हारा नाम]

hin_gr_5.what: अनुच्छेद लेखन एक मुख्य विचार के बारे में वाक्यों का समूह है, जिसमें विषय वाक्य, सहायक विवरण और निष्कर्ष होता है।
hin_gr_5.example: अनुच्छेद एक सैंडविच की तरह है: विषय वाक्य ऊपर की रोटी है, विवरण भरावन है, और निष्कर्ष नीचे की रोटी है!
points: स्पष्ट विषय वाक्य से शुरू करें जो मुख्य विचार बताए
3-5 सहायक वाक्य जोड़ें विवरण और उदाहरणों के साथ
निष्कर्ष वाक्य से समाप्त करें
hin_gr_5.tip: संक्रमण शब्दों का उपयोग करें जैसे 'पहले', 'इसके अलावा', 'अंत में' वाक्यों को जोड़ने के लिए!
hin_gr_5.question: एक अच्छे अनुच्छेद के तीन भाग क्या हैं?
tryIt: अपने पसंदीदा शौक के बारे में एक अनुच्छेद लिखें (5-6 वाक्य)।
विषय वाक्य को रेखांकित करें और निष्कर्ष को हाइलाइट करें।

comp_paint_2.what: Digital art is creating artwork using computer software and tools like MS Paint, allowing you to draw, color, and design on screen.
comp_paint_2.example: Just like painting on paper, but you can undo mistakes with one click!
points: Use drawing tools like pencil, brush, and spray paint for different effects
Fill tool helps color large areas quickly
You can save and edit your artwork anytime without wasting paper
comp_paint_2.tip: Experiment with different colors and tools — digital art lets you try unlimited ideas!
comp_paint_2.question: What are the advantages of digital art over traditional art?

activity.title: Create a Digital Greeting Card
materials: Computer with MS Paint or similar software
instructions: Open MS Paint and set canvas size to 800x600 pixels.
Use shapes tool to create a border and decorative elements.
Add text with a greeting message using the text tool.
Use colors and brushes to make it colorful and attractive.
Save your card and share it with family or friends.
activity.outcome: You'll create a personalized digital greeting card that can be printed or shared online.

tryIt: Draw your favorite cartoon character using MS Paint.
Create a poster about 'Save Water' with drawings and text.
Design a simple logo for your class or school club.

kan_lit_3.what: ಐಕ್ಯತೆ ಎಂದರೆ ಒಗ್ಗಟ್ಟು ಮತ್ತು ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆ — ಎಲ್ಲರೂ ಒಟ್ಟಾಗಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡುವುದು ಮತ್ತು ದೇಶಪ್ರೇಮದ ಸಂದೇಶ.
kan_lit_3.example: ಅನೇಕ ದಾರಗಳು ಸೇರಿ ಬಲವಾದ ಹಗ್ಗವಾಗುವಂತೆ, ಐಕ್ಯತೆಯಿಂದ ನಾವು ಬಲಶಾಲಿಯಾಗುತ್ತೇವೆ!
points: ವಿವಿಧ ಸಂಸ್ಕೃತಿಗಳು ಮತ್ತು ಭಾಷೆಗಳ ಜನರು ಒಟ್ಟಾಗಿ ವಾಸಿಸುವುದು
ಐಕ್ಯತೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಶಕ್ತಿ ಇದೆ
ದೇಶದ ಪ್ರಗತಿಗೆ ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆ ಅತ್ಯಗತ್ಯ
kan_lit_3.question: ಐಕ್ಯತೆ ಏಕೆ ಮುಖ್ಯ?
thinkDiscuss: ನಿಮ್ಮ ತರಗತಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ವಿವಿಧ ಹಿನ್ನೆಲೆಯ ವಿದ್ಯಾರ್ಥಿಗಳು ಹೇಗೆ ಒಟ್ಟಾಗಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡುತ್ತಾರೆ?
ಐಕ್ಯತೆಯನ್ನು ಬೆಳೆಸಲು ನಾವು ಏನು ಮಾಡಬಹುದು?

kan_pro_1.what: ಸ್ವರಾರ್ಪಣ ಎಂದರೆ ಸಂಗೀತ ಮತ್ತು ಕಲೆಯ ಮೂಲಕ ತನ್ನ ಭಾವನೆಗಳನ್ನು ವ್ಯಕ್ತಪಡಿಸುವುದು.
kan_pro_1.example: ಹಾಡು ಹಾಡುವಾಗ ನಿಮ್ಮ ಹೃದಯದ ಭಾವನೆಗಳು ಹೊರಬರುತ್ತವೆ!
points: ಸಂಗೀತವು ಸಾರ್ವತ್ರಿಕ ಭಾಷೆ
ಕಲೆ ಮತ್ತು ಸಂಗೀತ ಮನಸ್ಸಿಗೆ ಶಾಂತಿ ತರುತ್ತದೆ
ಅಭ್ಯಾಸದಿಂದ ಕಲೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಪರಿಣತಿ ಬರುತ್ತದೆ
kan_pro_1.tip: ಪ್ರತಿದಿನ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಸಮಯ ಸಂಗೀತ ಅಥವಾ ಕಲೆಗೆ ಮೀಸಲಿಡಿ!
kan_pro_1.question: ಸಂಗೀತ ಮತ್ತು ಕಲೆ ನಮ್ಮ ಜೀವನದಲ್ಲಿ ಏಕೆ ಮುಖ್ಯ?
tryIt: ನಿಮ್ಮ ನೆಚ್ಚಿನ ಹಾಡನ್ನು ಹಾಡಿ ಮತ್ತು ಅದರ ಭಾವನೆಯನ್ನು ಅನುಭವಿಸಿ.
ಸರಳ ಚಿತ್ರವನ್ನು ಬಿಡಿಸಿ ಮತ್ತು ಬಣ್ಣ ತುಂಬಿ.

kan_pro_2.what: ಧೀರ ಸೇನಾನಿ ಎಂದರೆ ದೇಶದ ರಕ್ಷಣೆಗಾಗಿ ಧೈರ್ಯದಿಂದ ಹೋರಾಡಿದ ವೀರ ಯೋಧನ ಕಥೆ.
kan_pro_2.example: ಸೈನಿಕರು ನಮ್ಮ ದೇಶವನ್ನು ರಕ್ಷಿಸಲು ತಮ್ಮ ಜೀವವನ್ನು ಅಪಾಯಕ್ಕೆ ಒಡ್ಡುತ್ತಾರೆ!
points: ಸೈನಿಕರು ದೇಶಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ತ್ಯಾಗ ಮಾಡುತ್ತಾರೆ
ಧೈರ್ಯ ಮತ್ತು ಶಿಸ್ತು ಸೈನಿಕರ ಗುಣಗಳು
ನಾವು ಸೈನಿಕರನ್ನು ಗೌರವಿಸಬೇಕು
kan_pro_2.tip: ದೇಶಪ್ರೇಮ ಎಂದರೆ ನಮ್ಮ ದೇಶವನ್ನು ಪ್ರೀತಿಸುವುದು ಮತ್ತು ಅದರ ಕಲ್ಯಾಣಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡುವುದು!
kan_pro_2.question: ಸೈನಿಕರು ನಮ್ಮ ದೇಶಕ್ಕೆ ಹೇಗೆ ಸೇವೆ ಸಲ್ಲಿಸುತ್ತಾರೆ?
thinkDiscuss: ಸೈನಿಕರ ಕುಟುಂಬಗಳು ಎದುರಿಸುವ ಸವಾಲುಗಳು ಯಾವುವು?
ನಾವು ಸೈನಿಕರಿಗೆ ಕೃತಜ್ಞತೆ ತೋರಿಸಲು ಏನು ಮಾಡಬಹುದು?

kan_gr_3.what: ಕ್ರಿಯಾಪದ ಎಂದರೆ ಕೆಲಸ ಅಥವಾ ಕ್ರಿಯೆಯನ್ನು ತಿಳಿಸುವ ಪದ. ಕಾಲಗಳು ಕ್ರಿಯೆ ಯಾವಾಗ ನಡೆಯುತ್ತದೆ ಎಂಬುದನ್ನು ತೋರಿಸುತ್ತವೆ.
kan_gr_3.example: ನಾನು ಓದುತ್ತೇನೆ (ವರ್ತಮಾನ), ನಾನು ಓದಿದೆ (ಭೂತ), ನಾನು ಓದುವೆನು (ಭವಿಷ್ಯತ್)
points: ವರ್ತಮಾನ ಕಾಲ: ಈಗ ನಡೆಯುತ್ತಿರುವ ಕ್ರಿಯೆ
ಭೂತಕಾಲ: ಮುಗಿದ ಕ್ರಿಯೆ
ಭವಿಷ್ಯತ್ ಕಾಲ: ಮುಂದೆ ನಡೆಯುವ ಕ್ರಿಯೆ
kan_gr_3.question: ಕ್ರಿಯಾಪದದ ಮೂರು ಕಾಲಗಳು ಯಾವುವು?
tryIt: ಈ ಕ್ರಿಯಾಪದಗಳನ್ನು ಮೂರು ಕಾಲಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಬರೆಯಿರಿ: ಓಡು, ತಿನ್ನು, ಬರೆ
ನಿಮ್ಮ ದಿನದ ಚಟುವಟಿಕೆಗಳನ್ನು ಕ್ರಿಯಾಪದಗಳೊಂದಿಗೆ ವಾಕ್ಯಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಬರೆಯಿರಿ.

examples.question: ವರ್ತಮಾನ ಕಾಲದ ಉದಾಹರಣೆಗಳು
steps: ನಾನು ಶಾಲೆಗೆ ಹೋಗುತ್ತೇನೆ.
ಅವಳು ಪುಸ್ತಕ ಓದುತ್ತಾಳೆ.
ಅವರು ಆಟವಾಡುತ್ತಾರೆ.

eng_sup_4.what: A kind shopkeeper helps a poor man fix his old clock, and in return receives an unexpected reward.
eng_sup_4.example: Have you ever helped someone without expecting anything in return?
points: The shopkeeper repairs the clock for free out of kindness
The old man turns out to be a wealthy person in disguise
Kindness and generosity are always rewarded
eng_sup_4.tip: True generosity means helping others without expecting rewards!
eng_sup_4.question: Why did the shopkeeper help the old man?
tryIt: Think of one act of kindness you can do today for someone in need.
Write a short paragraph about how helping others makes you feel.
Share your story with your classmates.
thinkDiscuss: Do you think the shopkeeper would have helped if he knew the man was wealthy?
What does this story teach us about judging people by their appearance?

eng_sup_5.what: Tansen was one of the greatest musicians in Emperor Akbar's court, famous for his magical voice and mastery of ragas.
eng_sup_5.example: Imagine a singer so talented that their music could light lamps or bring rain!
points: Tansen learned music from the great guru Swami Haridas
He could sing Raga Deepak to light lamps and Raga Megh Malhar to bring rain
His dedication and practice made him a legend
eng_sup_5.tip: Practice and dedication can turn talent into mastery!
eng_sup_5.question: Which raga did Tansen sing to bring rain?
tryIt: Listen to a classical Indian raga online and describe how it makes you feel.
Try humming a simple tune and practice it daily for a week.
Research one famous Indian classical musician and present their story.
thinkDiscuss: Why do you think music was so important in Emperor Akbar's court?
Can dedication and practice help you master any skill? Give examples.

eng_sup_6.what: A clever monkey outwits a crocodile who tries to trick him, teaching us about quick thinking and friendship.
eng_sup_6.example: Have you ever had to think quickly to get out of a tricky situation?
points: The crocodile befriends the monkey but plans to betray him
The monkey uses his intelligence to escape the crocodile's trap
True friendship is based on trust, not deception
eng_sup_6.tip: Quick thinking and presence of mind can save you from danger!
eng_sup_6.question: How did the monkey escape from the crocodile?
tryIt: Act out the story with your friends, taking turns as the monkey and crocodile.
Write an alternate ending where the crocodile learns his lesson.
Draw a comic strip showing the key moments of the story.
thinkDiscuss: Was the crocodile a true friend? Why or why not?
What would you do if a friend betrayed your trust?

eng_sup_7.what: Sleep is a natural state of rest that helps our body and brain recover, grow, and stay healthy.
eng_sup_7.example: After a long day of playing and studying, your body needs sleep to recharge like a phone battery!
points: During sleep, our body repairs tissues and grows
Dreams occur during REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep
Lack of sleep affects memory, mood, and health
eng_sup_7.tip: Get 8-10 hours of sleep every night to stay healthy and alert!
eng_sup_7.question: Why is sleep important for our body?

activity.title: Sleep Diary Experiment
instructions: For one week, record what time you go to bed and wake up.
Note how you feel each morning (energetic, tired, sleepy).
Calculate your total sleep hours each night.
Compare days when you slept well vs. poorly.
activity.outcome: You will discover how sleep affects your energy and mood!

tryIt: Create a bedtime routine that helps you sleep better.
Avoid screens 30 minutes before bed and see if you sleep better.
Share your sleep diary findings with your class.
thinkDiscuss: What happens to your body when you don't get enough sleep?
Why do we dream? What do you think dreams mean?

eng_sup_8.what: A brave girl makes a pact with the sun to help her sick mother recover by bringing sunlight into their dark home.
eng_sup_8.example: Have you ever wished you could talk to nature to solve a problem?
points: The girl's mother is sick because their house gets no sunlight
She bravely asks the sun for help
The sun agrees to shine into their home, and her mother recovers
eng_sup_8.tip: Courage and determination can help us overcome any obstacle!
eng_sup_8.question: Why was the girl's mother sick?
tryIt: Observe how sunlight enters different rooms in your house throughout the day.
Write a letter to the sun, moon, or stars expressing your feelings.
Create a poster showing the importance of sunlight for health.
thinkDiscuss: How does sunlight affect our health and mood?
What would you do if someone you love was sick?

eng_gr_8.what: A paragraph is a group of sentences about one main idea, with a topic sentence, supporting details, and a concluding sentence.
eng_gr_8.example: If you're writing about your favorite sport, all sentences in the paragraph should relate to that sport.
points: Start with a topic sentence that states the main idea
Add 3-5 supporting sentences with details and examples
